ogTitle: Burleigh Waters vs Burncluith: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burleigh Waters and Burncluith.
heading: Burleigh Waters vs Burncluith — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burleigh Waters, Queensland and Burncluith, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burncluith has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Burleigh Waters (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burleigh Waters or Burncluith safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burleigh Waters has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Burncluith scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burleigh Waters scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Burncluith scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burleigh Waters or Burncluith?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burncluith has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
